The latest update is out from Fidelity National Info ( (FIS) ).

On March 30, 2026, Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. announced that board member Mark Benjamin has chosen not to stand for re-election at the company’s 2026 annual meeting of shareholders, with the company emphasizing that his decision was not due to any disagreement over operations, policies, or practices. Following Benjamin’s departure after the 2026 shareholder meeting, the board will be reduced from ten to nine directors, a move that slightly streamlines governance but maintains board continuity while the company publicly recognizes his significant contributions to the evolution and positioning of its business.

The most recent analyst rating on (FIS) stock is a Hold with a $52.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fidelity National Info stock, see the FIS Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FIS is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by strong cash generation and a de-risked balance sheet, reinforced by upbeat guidance for revenue/EBITDA, margin expansion, and higher free cash flow. These positives are tempered by weak technical trends, a very high P/E that embeds optimistic expectations, and execution risk from integration costs and leadership change.

To see Spark’s full report on FIS stock, click here.

More about Fidelity National Info

Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. is a financial technology company that provides software, processing, and technology solutions to financial institutions, merchants, and capital markets firms. Its offerings support core banking, payments, securities processing, and related services, positioning the company as a major infrastructure provider in the global financial services industry.

data.title: SBA Default on Debentures Puts Medallion Financial Under Review
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Medallion Financial ( (MFIN) ) has provided an update.

As of March 31, 2026, approximately 3% of Medallion Financial Corp.’s consolidated borrowings, totaling $73.5 million, consisted of SBA debentures issued to its subsidiary Medallion Capital, Inc., providing long-term, fixed-rate funding at relatively low cost. On that date, Medallion Capital submitted a management team for review to the SBA’s licensing division, but shortly afterward the SBA declared an event of default on the debentures, finding Medallion Capital lacked a qualified management team and directing it to identify and submit at least one additional qualified candidate for a full-time principal and investment committee role within 120 days.

The SBA’s default notice does not trigger cross-defaults under Medallion Financial Corp.’s other debt agreements, limiting immediate spillover risk for the parent company’s broader capital structure. However, if Medallion Capital fails to meet the SBA’s requirements, the agency may accelerate repayment of the debentures and accrued interest or pursue other remedies under the Small Business Investment Act of 1958, which could raise funding costs and pressure liquidity, even as Medallion Capital continues working to supplement its management submission and asserts that, with the planned changes, it will have a qualified team.

The most recent analyst rating on (MFIN) stock is a Hold with a $8.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Medallion Financial stock, see the MFIN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MFIN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MFIN is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by reasonably strong financial performance with some volatility risk, supported by very attractive valuation (low P/E and strong dividend). These positives are tempered by weak technical momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD) and earnings-call risks around credit costs and potential margin pressure despite constructive growth guidance.

To see Spark’s full report on MFIN stock, click here.

More about Medallion Financial

Medallion Financial Corp., through its subsidiary Medallion Capital, Inc., operates in the financial services industry, using debenture financing from the U.S. Small Business Administration to support its lending and investment activities. These SBA debentures provide the company with long-term, typically 10-year, fixed-rate funding at coupons that are generally lower than other available capital sources, representing a cost-effective component of its consolidated borrowings.

Average Trading Volume: 54,917

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $199.3M

For an in-depth examination of MFIN stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

Newtek Business ( (NEWT) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 1, 2026, NewtekOne, Inc. renewed for an additional year the employment agreements of Chief Executive Officer and President Barry Sloane, Chief Legal Officer and Corporate Secretary Michael A. Schwartz, and Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President Frank DeMaria. Newtek Bank, N.A., the company’s subsidiary, also renewed for one year the employment agreement of its president, Peter Downs.

The terms of Sloane’s agreement remain unchanged from the prior arrangement, while the renewed agreements for Downs, Schwartz, and DeMaria now include a non-renewal payment equal to one year of base compensation. The move underscores NewtekOne’s emphasis on continuity in its leadership team and provides greater clarity around executive retention and exit economics for key stakeholders.

data.title: Denali Regains DNL593 Rights After Takeda Collaboration Ends
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Denali Therapeutics ( (DNLI) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 3, 2026, Denali Therapeutics said Takeda Pharmaceutical will terminate their 2018 collaboration to co-develop and co-commercialize DNL593, an investigational progranulin replacement therapy for GRN-related frontotemporal dementia. The decision, described as strategic and not data-driven, will take effect 60 days after notice, at which point Denali regains full global rights and intellectual property and has no further financial obligations to Takeda.

Denali, which has led development of DNL593, plans to continue the program independently and report results from its ongoing Phase 1/2 study in FTD-GRN by the end of 2026. The trial has fully enrolled 40 patients, and earlier interim data from healthy volunteers showed dose-dependent increases in cerebrospinal fluid progranulin and no significant safety signals, reinforcing Denali’s positioning around its clinically validated TransportVehicle blood-brain barrier-crossing technology in a field with no approved disease-modifying FTD treatments.

The most recent analyst rating on (DNLI) stock is a Hold with a $19.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Denali Therapeutics stock, see the DNLI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DNLI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DNLI is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (widening losses, cash burn, and recent $0 revenue) and bearish technical signals. These are partly offset by strong, positive corporate catalysts (FDA accelerated approval and $200M non-dilutive funding) and a low-leverage balance sheet.

To see Spark’s full report on DNLI stock, click here.

More about Denali Therapeutics

Denali Therapeutics Inc. is a biotechnology company focused on developing biotherapeutics that cross the blood-brain barrier using its proprietary TransportVehicle platform. The company is building a portfolio of candidates targeting neurodegenerative diseases, lysosomal storage disorders and other serious conditions with high unmet medical need.

Average Trading Volume: 1,810,469

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $3.27B

Learn more about DNLI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

The latest announcement is out from AMC Networks ( (AMCX) ).

AMC Networks Inc., a U.S. entertainment and television content provider, reported a leadership change in its finance organization effective March 31, 2026. The company, which operates cable networks and streaming services with a focus on premium and niche programming, continues to emphasize stability in its financial oversight structure.

On March 31, 2026, the previously announced resignation of Chief Financial Officer Patrick O’Connell took effect, with the company stating his departure was not due to any disagreement over operations, policies or practices. In connection with his exit, Chief Accounting Officer Michael J. Sherin III assumed the role of principal financial officer on an interim basis, signaling a transitional phase in the company’s senior financial leadership while maintaining continuity in fiscal management.

The most recent analyst rating on (AMCX) stock is a Hold with a $6.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on AMC Networks stock, see the AMCX Stock Forecast page.

AMCX scores mid-range primarily due to resilient cash generation and a profitability rebound, partially offset by ongoing multi-year revenue decline and leverage risk. Technicals are notably weak and weigh on the score, while earnings-call guidance points to near-term profitability and cash flow pressure despite continued progress on streaming mix shift and debt reduction.

To see Spark’s full report on AMCX stock, click here.

More about AMC Networks

data.title: Anand Rathi Wealth Files SEBI Demat Compliance Certificate for March 2026 Quarter
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Anand Rathi Wealth Ltd. ( (IN:ANANDRATHI) ).

Anand Rathi Wealth Limited has submitted a compliance certificate to the National Stock Exchange of India and BSE for the quarter ended 31 March 2026, in line with Regulation 74(5) of SEBI’s Depositories and Participants Regulations, 2018. The certificate, issued by its registrar and share transfer agent MUFG Intime India, confirms that securities received for dematerialisation during the quarter were processed, verified, mutilated and cancelled as required, with depositories correctly recorded as registered owners within prescribed timelines.

This routine filing underscores the company’s adherence to regulatory norms governing dematerialisation and listing of securities on stock exchanges. The confirmation supports transparency and operational integrity in its share transfer and custody processes, providing reassurance to regulators, investors and other stakeholders about proper handling of its equity securities.

More about Anand Rathi Wealth Ltd.

Anand Rathi Wealth Limited is a Mumbai-based wealth management company focused on private wealth services. The firm operates as an AMFI-registered mutual fund distributor, offering advisory and distribution solutions to high-net-worth and other individual investors in India’s financial markets.
